structured_payload_candidate.title: Override Capability for All Points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this, equate to a software point and use the software point in all sequences.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Ensure all points can be overridden; if hardware points cannot be overridden, equate them to software points and use the software points in sequences.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify that all points can be overridden by the user with appropriate access level.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this for hardware points, they shall be equated to a software point, and the software point shall be used in all sequences.

evidence_quote: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this for hardware points, they shall be equated to a software point, and the software point shall be used in all sequences.
evidence_text: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this for hardware points, they shall be equated to a software point, and the software point shall be used in all sequences.
judge_reason: Commissioning step for override capability, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Enable/Disable to Prevent Windup
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable or disable control loops based on the status of the system being controlled.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.

evidence_quote: Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Initialization to Neutral Value
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents shall be set initially to a neutral value.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set the control loop and all its constituents (proportional and integral terms) to a neutral value.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value.

evidence_quote: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value.
evidence_text: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value.
judge_reason: Operational sequence for control loop initialization, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Neutral Control Loop Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply the minimum control effect: valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed, etc.

evidence_quote: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed, etc.
evidence_text: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed, etc.
judge_reason: Definition of neutral control loop condition with operational implications, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Validity
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and unit in Occupied Mode or economizer enabled
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Consider the sensor valid only under the specified conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.

evidence_quote: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.

judge_reason: Sensor validity rule with operational conditions, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Global Outdoor Air Temperature Calculation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid sensors, discard the highest and lowest readings.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Calculating outdoor air temperature for global sequences
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Average all valid sensor readings; if four or more valid sensors, discard highest and lowest before averaging.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.

evidence_quote: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.
evidence_text: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.
judge_reason: Global outdoor air temperature calculation method for control sequences, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Software Point and Software Switch Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A 'software point' is an analog variable, and a 'software switch' is a digital (binary) variable, not associated with real I/O points. They shall be read/write capable.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use read/write capable analog variables for software points and binary variables for software switches.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The term 'software point' shall mean an analog variable, and 'software switch' shall mean a digital (binary) variable, that are not associated with real I/O points. They shall be read/write capable (e.g., BACnet analog variable and binary variable).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Proportional-Only Loops for Limiting Loops
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Limiting control loops (e.g., zone CO2 control)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Use proportional only (P-only) loops for limiting loops to prevent integral windup.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Derivative Term Usage Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Adjustability of Setpoints, Timers, Deadbands, PID Gains
structured_payload_candidate.summary: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains, etc. listed in sequences shall be adjustable by the user with appropriate access level. Software points shall be used for these variables. Fixed scalar numbers shall not be embedded in programs except for physical constants and conversion factors.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maximum Rate of Change for Control Loop Output
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Limit the rate of change of the control loop output to a user-adjustable maximum, default 25% per minute.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Mode Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operators shall have the ability to put any device in/out of maintenance mode. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed, except life safety alarms. If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating the device is still in maintenance mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress all alarms except life safety; issue a daily Level 3 alarm while in maintenance mode.

If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Post-Exit Suppression Period Defaults
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Default suppression periods: Level 1 alarms: 0 minutes, Level 2 alarms: 5 minutes, Level 3 alarms: 24 hours, Level 4 alarms: 7 days.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Equipment Staging and Rotation Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Parallel equipment shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Rotate lead/lag or lead/standby to maintain even wear.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Parallel equipment shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Lag Equipment Rotation Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Identical parallel staged equipment (e.g., CHW pumps, cooling towers)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: More than one equipment off or more than one on
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/lag based on Staging Runtime: most hours to last stage, least hours to lead stage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.

evidence_quote: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.
evidence_text: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.
judge_reason: Lead/lag rotation logic with staging runtime, fully supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Faulted Equipment Definition and Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A faulted equipment is any equipment commanded to run that is either not running or unable to perform its required duty. If an operating equipment has any fault condition described, a Level 2 alarm shall be generated and a response shall be triggered.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Equipment commanded to run but not running or unable to perform duty
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger response as defined.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A faulted equipment is any equipment commanded to run that is either not running or unable to perform its required duty. If an operating equipment has any fault condition described subsequently, a Level 2 alarm shall be generated and a response shall be triggered as defined below.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan and Pump Fault Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: After 15 seconds delay, if status does not match command for 3 seconds, generate Level 2 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Equipment Rotation (Non-Continuous)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel equipment that are 100% redundant and do not run continuously
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/standby based on Staging Runtime: most hours to last stage, least hours to earlier stage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.

evidence_quote: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.
evidence_text: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.
judge_reason: Lead/standby rotation for non-continuous equipment, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Variable Speed Fan/Pump Deceleration Rate
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Disabling variable speed fan or pump
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set deceleration rate to 1 Hz/second or slower.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment (e.g., chillers).

evidence_quote: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment (e.g., chillers).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Rotation for Continuously Running Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Start and prove standby equipment on before shutting off former lead equipment.
configurable_values: Day of week and time for switch
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.

evidence_quote: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.
evidence_text: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.
judge_reason: Lead/standby rotation for continuously running equipment, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Operator Override of Staging Order
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operators with appropriate access level shall be able to manually command staging order via software points, but not overriding the In-Alarm or Hand-Operation logic. Staging order changes initiated via operator override shall be instituted as part of normal staging events. Staging order shall remain overridden until released by operators.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Allow manual staging order override via software points; implement changes during normal staging events; maintain override until released.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Operators with appropriate access level shall be able to manually command staging order via software points, but not overriding the In-Alarm or Hand-Operation logic in the following subsections. 1. Staging order changes initiated via operator override shall be instituted as part of normal staging events. 2. Staging order shall remain overridden until released by operators.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK TRUE Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: the system is proven ON, the system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes, and the system is ready and able to serve its load.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SystemOK to TRUE only when all conditions are met.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hierarchical Alarm Suppression Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The BAS shall selectively suppress alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. For cooling water systems, suppress only high-temperature alarms. For heating water systems, suppress only low-temperature alarms. For air-side systems, suppress all alarms from loads.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: SystemOK is FALSE for a source system
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress alarms for load components as specified based on source system type.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.

evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.

judge_reason: Hierarchical alarm suppression logic, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cascading Hierarchical Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Cascade suppression to downstream loads.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarms Never Suppressed by Hierarchical Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The following types of alarms will never be suppressed: life/safety and Level 1 alarms, failure-to-start alarms, and failure-to-stop/hand alarms.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Never suppress life/safety, Level 1, failure-to-start, or failure-to-stop/hand alarms.
